definitions: People who are kind, cooperative, and law-abiding are often not successful in life, especially in competitive situations, because they are exploited or outmaneuvered by more aggressive or ruthless individuals.
Being too agreeable or ethical can be a disadvantage in achieving success or power.
examples: He always tried to be fair, but in business, nice guys finish last.
She felt that in the corporate world, nice guys finish last, so she started to be more assertive.
The coach told his team that sometimes nice guys finish last, implying they needed to be tougher.
